Bash(cd "C:/Users/Micha/OneDrive/Dokumente/claude_code/Aftersales/django" && docker compose exec backend python manage.py shell -c "
from apps.users.models import User, Membership
from apps.tenants.models import Tenant
from apps.billing.models import TenantSubscription
from apps.audit.services import AuditService

# Superuser
admin = User.objects.create_superuser\(email='admin@aftersales.dev', password='admin123', first_name='Admin', last_name='User'\)

# 3 Demo-Tenants
t1 = Tenant.objects.create\(name='Digitalagentur Weber', slug='digitalagentur-weber'\)
t2 = Tenant.objects.create\(name='Marketing Meier GmbH', slug='marketing-meier'\)
t3 = Tenant.objects.create\(name='Webdesign Schmidt', slug='webdesign-schmidt'\)

# Admin ist Owner von allen 3
Membership.objects.create\(user=admin, tenant=t1, role='owner'\)
Membership.objects.create\(user=admin, tenant=t2, role='owner'\)
Membership.objects.create\(user=admin, tenant=t3, role='owner'\)

# Weitere User
u1 = User.objects.create_user\(email='sarah@weber.de', password='demo1234', first_name='Sarah', last_name='Weber'\)
u2 = User.objects.create_user\(email='tom@meier.de', password='demo1234', first_name='Tom', last_name='Meier'\)
u3 = User.objects.create_user\(email='lisa@schmidt.de', password='demo1234', first_name='Lisa', last_name='Schmidt'\)
u4 = User.objects.create_user\(email='max@weber.de', password='demo1234', first_name='Max', last_name='Mueller'\)

# Memberships
Membership.objects.create\(user=u1, tenant=t1, role='admin'\)
Membership.objects.create\(user=u4, tenant=t1, role='member'\)
Membership.objects.create\(user=u2, tenant=t2, role='admin'\)
Membership.objects.create\(user=u3, tenant=t3, role='admin'\)
Membership.objects.create\(user=u3, tenant=t1, role='member'\)  # Lisa ist auch bei Weber

# Subscriptions
TenantSubscription.objects.create\(tenant=t1, status='active', plan_name='Professional'\)
TenantSubscription.objects.create\(tenant=t2, status='trialing', plan_name='Starter'\)
TenantSubscription.objects.create\(tenant=t3, status='none'\)

# Audit Events
AuditService.log\(tenant=t1, user=admin, action='tenant.created', entity_type='tenant', entity_id=str\(t1.id\), after={'name': t1.name}\)
AuditService.log\(tenant=t1, user=admin, action='member.invited', entity_type='membership', entity_id='demo', after={'email': 'sarah@weber.de', 'role': 'admin'}\)
AuditService.log\(tenant=t1, user=u1, action='member.invited', entity_type='membership', entity_id='demo', after={'email': 'max@weber.de', 'role': 'member'}\)
AuditService.log\(tenant=t2, user=admin, action='tenant.created', entity_type='tenant', entity_id=str\(t2.id\), after={'name': t2.name}\)

print\('--- Demo-Daten erstellt ---'\)
print\(f'Users: {User.objects.count\(\)}'\)
print\(f'Tenants: {Tenant.objects.count\(\)}'\)
print\(f'Memberships: {Membership.objects.count\(\)}'\)
print\(f'Subscriptions: {TenantSubscription.objects.count\(\)}'\)
print\(f'Audit Events: {AuditService.__module__}'\)
" 2>&1)

Bash(cd "C:/Users/Micha/OneDrive/Dokumente/claude_code/Aftersales/django" && docker compose exec backend python manage.py shell -c "
from allauth.account.models import EmailAddress
from apps.users.models import User

for user in User.objects.all\(\):
    EmailAddress.objects.get_or_create\(
        user=user,
        email=user.email,
        defaults={'verified': True, 'primary': True},
    \)
    # Falls schon vorhanden, auf verified setzen
    EmailAddress.objects.filter\(user=user, email=user.email\).update\(verified=True, primary=True\)

print\(f'Verifiziert: {EmailAddress.objects.filter\(verified=True\).count\(\)} Emails'\)
" 2>&1)
